name: Mountain West Cu Associates
description_short: GoWest Association (formerly Mountain West CU Associates) is a six-state trade organization and advocacy body serving over 300 credit unions and 17.4 million members across the western United States.
description_long: GoWest Association is a regional credit union league and trade organization that represents and supports credit unions across six western states. The organization was previously known as Mountain West Credit Union Associates, as reflected in the domain mwcua.com. Operating as a non-profit cooperative itself, GoWest serves as the primary advocacy, education, and support infrastructure for member credit unions and their leadership. The organization provides a comprehensive suite of services designed to strengthen credit union operations and leadership. These include advocacy and regulatory compliance support, professional development councils covering eight different focus areas, community impact initiatives, and peer-learning forums. GoWest hosts major conferences like VisionBoard for credit union board leaders and CEOs, operates regional chapters (such as Valley of the Sun and King County chapters), and publishes educational content on topics ranging from AI implementation to financial wellness data. The organization also maintains resource centers and facilitates connections between credit union professionals through programs like the Young Credit Union Professionals (YCUP) initiative. GoWest distinguishes itself as a mission-driven organization focused on promoting the credit union movement's cooperative, not-for-profit model. The organization emphasizes advocacy work to demonstrate how credit unions differ from traditional banks and banking institutions, positioning them as community-focused alternatives that prioritize member welfare over shareholder returns. Their eight councils and multiple chapter structures create localized networks while maintaining a unified six-state presence, allowing for both national-level advocacy and regional relevance. It's important to note that GoWest Association is NOT a direct financial services provider to consumers. This is a B2B trade organization serving credit unions themselves, not individual borrowers or depositors. While the organization supports credit unions that may offer loans, savings accounts, and other consumer financial products, GoWest does not directly issue credit cards, process loans, manage deposits, or provide any retail banking services. Consumers seeking banking products would need to work with individual credit unions that are GoWest members.
address: 650 S Cherry St Suite 920, Denver, CO 80246

company_info.headquarters: 650 S Cherry St Suite 920, Denver, CO 80246

pros: Serves over 300 credit unions representing 17.4 million consumer members across six states
Offers eight specialized councils with dedicated focus areas including new Professional Development Council
Hosts VisionBoard conference bringing together credit union board leaders and CEOs for peer learning
Provides advocacy and compliance support helping member credit unions navigate regulatory landscape
Operates regional chapters enabling localized networking and connection opportunities
Publishes original financial wellness research and scorecards (e.g., Oregon Financial Wellness Scorecard)
Supports emerging leaders through Young Credit Union Professionals (YCUP) program
cons: Not a direct financial services provider—cannot serve individual consumers seeking loans or accounts
Website content focuses heavily on internal organization events rather than clear explanation of member benefits
Limited transparency about specific advocacy wins or regulatory impact outcomes on the public website
Membership and participation primarily limited to credit union institutions, not retail consumers
Website navigation and structure could be clearer for stakeholders trying to understand services offered
best_for: Credit union boards and executive leadership seeking professional development and peer networks
Credit union compliance and operations teams needing advocacy support and regulatory guidance
Young professionals entering the credit union industry looking for mentorship and career development
Credit unions seeking market research and financial wellness data for their regions
diagnosis: GoWest Association is a B2B trade organization serving credit union institutions and their leadership, not a direct financial services provider to consumers. Individuals seeking banking products, loans, or credit union membership should contact specific credit unions in their region, many of which may be GoWest members. This organization is best for credit union professionals, board members, and executives seeking industry support, advocacy, education, and peer networking.
services: Advocacy and regulatory compliance support for member credit unions
Eight specialized councils covering different operational and strategic focus areas
VisionBoard annual conference for credit union board leaders and CEOs
Regional chapter operations and networking events (Valley of the Sun, King County, etc.)
Young Credit Union Professionals (YCUP) development and networking program
CU Communities Huddle biweekly connection forums for chapter and board members
Educational webinars on emerging topics (AI, digital services, conflict management)
Financial wellness research and state-level economic data analysis
Resource centers providing credit union education and industry information
Communication and messaging support for promoting credit union cooperative model
Foundation programs supporting community impact initiatives
Reporter resources and public education about credit unions
meta_description: GoWest Association serves over 300 credit unions and 17.4 million members across six western states as a trade and advocacy organization.
